2605.18732.title: Predictable Confabulations: Factual Recall by LLMs Scales with Model Size and Topic Frequency
2605.18732.paper_summary: While scaling laws govern aggregate large language model performance, no scaling law has linked factual recall to both model size and training-data composition. We evaluated 38 models on over 8,900 scholarly references evaluated by an automated reference verification system. Recall quality follows a sigmoid in the log-linear combination of model parameter count and topic representation in training data. These two variables alone explain 60% of the variance across 16 dense models from four families, rising to 74-94% within individual families. The form matches a superposition-inspired account in which recall is gated by a signal-to-noise ratio: signal strength scales with concept frequency and the noise floor with model capacity.
2605.18732.paper_summary_zh: 雖然縮放法則支配著大型語言模型的整體性能，但尚未有縮放法則將事實回憶與模型大小和訓練數據組成聯繫起來。<lb/>我們評估了38個模型，基於超過8,900個學術參考文獻，這些文獻由自動參考驗證系統進行評估。<lb/>回憶質量在模型參數數量和訓練數據中的主題表示的對數-線性組合中遵循S型曲線。<lb/>這兩個變量單獨解釋了來自四個家族的16個密集模型中60%的變異性，在個別家族中上升至74-94%。<lb/>這種形式與一種受疊加啟發的解釋相符，其中回憶受到信噪比的限制：信號強度隨著概念頻率的增加而增強，而噪聲底線則隨著模型容量的增加而變化。
2605.18732.author: Matthew L. Smith et.al.
2605.18732.authors: Matthew L. Smith, Jonathan P. Shock, Samuel T. Segun, Iyiola E. Olatunji, Tegawendé F. Bissyandé
